what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment associated with the design-source channel memory, linking an attachment id to an image:// target URI and a creation timestamp.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/vt-architects/p-1.
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
The file name is att-31930e8d.md matching the id field.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
References external assets via a custom image:// URI scheme.
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
concerns: No message or conversation id indicates what this attachment is attached to.
No uploader, author, or provenance metadata.
No alt text, caption, MIME type, dimensions, or file size.
The image:// URI resolution mechanism is not defined in this file.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points to a design-source image asset but carries little descriptive context.
questions: Which channel message or thread does this attachment belong to?
What does the image://design-source/vt-architects/p-1 URI resolve to?
Who added this attachment and why?
What is the actual content of the image?
Is vt-architects a project, team, or folder namespace?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file acts as a metadata record for an image attachment in channel memory, which is what its structure suggests.
intention_signals: To catalog an image attachment so the channel memory system can reference it by a stable id and a target URI.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to reference a design-source image asset rather than a conventional file path or URL.
contribution: A stable, id-based pointer from a channel attachment record to an image asset, preserving channel context and creation timestamp for retrieval and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record that maps attachment id att-31930e8d to the image target image://design-source/vt-architects/p-1 under the design-source channel.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to register an image attachment in the design-source channel memory for later reference and provenance.
